title: Velvety Citrus Kenyan Olive Stew
description: A fragrant, citrus‑infused one‑pot stew brimming with black olives, fresh veggies, and a velvety sauce that’s both egg‑free and vegan.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic, sautéing until the onion is translucent, about 3–4 minutes.
Stir in the sliced carrots, cubed sweet potato, and chopped kale; cook for another 2 minutes, letting the vegetables soften slightly.
Add the diced tomato and cook until it starts to break down, about 5 minutes.
Pour in the vegetable broth and add the lemon and orange juice, zest, cumin, coriander, turmeric, and chili flakes; bring to a gentle boil.
Add the halved olives, salt, and pepper, then reduce heat to low and let the stew simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Once the vegetables are tender and the sauce has thickened into a velvety consistency, taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Stir in the chopped fresh cilantro just before serving.
Serve hot, optionally with a side of steamed rice or flatbread.
Enjoy the bright citrus notes paired with the savory olives in this comforting stew.
